title: ‘End of an era’: Former world No. 1 Jamie Murray, Andy Murray’s brother, retires after 36-year career with seven Grand Slams
summary_full: Jamie Murray has retired from professional tennis at 40, concluding a distinguished doubles career spanning over three decades. A former world No. 1, he won seven Grand Slam titles, 34 ATP trophies and played a key role in Britain’s 2015 Davis Cup triumph. Renowned for his longevity and net play, Murray leaves a lasting legacy in British tennis.
